steps.title: Identify the context for the underlined phrase
steps.content: John “attempted to reconcile his mother to the notion” but found it difficult. Martha responded with self-pity and tears because his desires were incomprehensible to her. John is trying to get past her opposition to an idea she rejects.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Get his mother to accept’: “Reconcile (a person) to” means to cause them to accept something disagreeable — exactly what John is attempting against her resistance. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Get his mother to apologize for’: John wants her to accept his desire to travel, not apologize for it; there is no sense of blame here. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Get his mother to match’: The text never implies John wants his mother to mirror or equal his desire; he just wants her acceptance. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Get his mother to reunite with’: “Reunite with” implies a prior union that was broken; the text gives no indication she ever accepted the idea before. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
